blog_posts.title: Measuring data cleanup instead of trusting it
blog_posts.description: Most cleanup scripts fix messy exports with no way to know what they caught, missed, or silently broke. FDE Data Forge ends every run with a scorecard scored against row-level ground truth: 100% recall and 84.2% F1 on the bundled sample, with false positives shown rather than hidden. A walkthrough of the ingest-detect-normalize-validate-report pipeline and why measured accuracy matters.

blog_posts.title: Smaller chunks did not mean better retrieval
blog_posts.description: RAG Eval Bench measures a retrieval pipeline instead of eyeballing it: Hit@K, MRR, faithfulness, and correct-refusal rates from local Ollama models, every number copied from a committed artifact. The interesting result: shrinking chunks to 128 characters made retrieval worse, because most part descriptions are short and 128 fragments them. Evidence beats intuition.

blog_posts.title: Guardrails for AI coding agents that run offline
blog_posts.description: Agent Security Kit is a set of reusable guardrails for AI-assisted coding: secret-leak blocking, dangerous-command hooks, publish-readiness gates, and encrypted agent memory. Pure Bash, no SaaS scanner, no telemetry. A look at the failure modes of agent-driven development and the small, composable defenses that address them.
